ingredients.reason: Prescription diabetes medication illegally added to weight loss supplements
ingredients.mechanism_of_harm: Prescription diabetes medication (biguanide) illegally added to weight loss supplements. Causes dangerous hypoglycemia (low blood sugar - can be fatal), lactic acidosis (potentially fatal), and vitamin B12 deficiency. Requires medical monitoring. Found in numerous recalled weight loss products.

ingredients.reason: GHB precursor - metabolizes to date rape drug, causes overdose and death
ingredients.mechanism_of_harm: Industrial solvent that is metabolized by alcohol dehydrogenase and aldehyde dehydrogenase into gamma-hydroxybutyrate (GHB), a Schedule I controlled substance. Causes CNS depression, respiratory depression, unconsciousness, seizures, coma, and death. Synergistic with alcohol - extremely dangerous combination. Used as date rape drug precursor.

regulatory_status.US: FDA banned in dietary supplements. DEA List I chemical. Federal Analogue Act applies when sold for human consumption. Schedule I controlled substance analogue.
regulatory_status.DEA: List I chemical (GHB precursor). Schedule I analogue when intended for human consumption.
regulatory_status.States: Controlled in many states as GHB analogue

ingredients.notes: EXTREMELY DANGEROUS. Converts to GHB in body. Previously sold in supplements marketed for bodybuilding, sleep, and 'euphoria'. Multiple deaths reported. Dangerous with alcohol (blocks metabolism, prolongs effects). Products sold as 'Revitalize Plus', 'Serenity', 'Enliven', 'GHRE'. FDA/DEA joint enforcement actions.
scientific_references: FDA: Warning - GHB-related products in dietary supplements

ingredients.mechanism_of_harm: Potent mu-opioid receptor agonist with 13x higher potency than morphine. Causes respiratory depression, severe physical dependence, opioid-like withdrawal, and overdose risk. More potent than the parent kratom alkaloid mitragynine.

Not lawful in dietary supplements or food. Louisiana kratom ban effective August 1, 2025.

ingredients.reason: Contains monacolin K (chemically identical to lovastatin, a prescription statin drug). FDA considers RYR with significant monacolin K an unapproved new drug. EFSA 2025: no safe daily intake level for monacolins can be established.
ingredients.mechanism_of_harm: Monacolin K is lovastatin. Carries all statin adverse effects without pharmaceutical quality controls: hepatotoxicity (liver damage), myopathy/rhabdomyolysis (skeletal muscle breakdown), nephrotoxicity. The 2024 Kobayashi Pharmaceutical scandal (contamination with puberulic acid) caused up to 120 deaths and 500+ hospitalizations. Citrinin mycotoxin contamination is common in RYR products.

regulatory_status.US: FDA considers RYR with pharmacologically significant monacolin K an unapproved new drug (since 1998). Multiple warning letters issued.
regulatory_status.EU: EFSA 2025: no safe dose for monacolins. Commission revoked health claim July 2024. Total EU ban expected 2025.
regulatory_status.Australia: Listed as S4 controlled substance (prescription-only). Cannot be sold as supplement.
regulatory_status.Switzerland: Sales of red yeast rice are illegal.
regulatory_status.Canada: Permits up to 1 mg monacolins/day with mandatory warnings.

ingredients.notes: FDA has treated RYR with monacolin K as an illegal unapproved drug since 1998. EFSA (Jan 2025) concluded no safe daily intake level exists for monacolins from RYR. Australia classifies it as prescription-only (S4). Switzerland banned it entirely. The 2024 Kobayashi scandal caused up to 120 deaths from contaminated RYR. Multiple published cases of rhabdomyolysis and liver failure.

ingredients.reason: Severe hepatotoxicity. 1 death, 9 liver transplants, 200+ adverse liver events documented. FDA recalled Hydroxycut products (2009). France banned all GC supplements (2025). Australia mandated liver harm warnings (2025).
ingredients.mechanism_of_harm: Causes clinically apparent acute liver injury ranging from moderate transaminase elevations to fulminant hepatic failure requiring liver transplantation. Mechanism unclear but likely direct hepatotoxicity from HCA or contaminants. 91% hospitalization rate in DILIN study of 22 cases. Also associated with serotonin toxicity, mania, and pancreatitis.

regulatory_status.US: FDA recalled Hydroxycut products containing garcinia (2009). NCCIH warns it may be unsafe. Not banned as standalone ingredient but under scrutiny.
regulatory_status.France: Full 1-year ban on import, marketing, and sale (ministerial order April 15, 2025) after fatal fulminant hepatitis case. ANSES warned consumers not to consume it.
regulatory_status.Australia: Mandatory liver harm warning on all products containing GC or HCA, effective March 1, 2025.
regulatory_status.EU: EFSA ongoing safety assessment, EU-wide restrictions expected.

ingredients.notes: NIH LiverTox: 'Weight loss products labeled as containing Garcinia cambogia have been linked to development of clinically apparent acute liver injury which can be severe and even fatal.' Systematic review (2025) found 200+ adverse events, 34 published case reports, 1 death, 9 liver transplants. France banned all GC supplements April 2025 after fatal fulminant hepatitis. Australia mandated liver harm warnings March 2025. Banned in France, restricted in Australia and South Korea.

ingredients.reason: Classified as a controlled anabolic steroid precursor in multiple countries. Banned in UK, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand. Not approved as a dietary supplement outside the US.
ingredients.mechanism_of_harm: Metabolite of DHEA with androgenic/anabolic steroid activity. Despite marketing as 'non-hormonal', it is a prohormone that modulates cortisol metabolism and thyroid function. Potential for hormonal disruption with long-term use.

regulatory_status.US: Legal as dietary supplement. FDA has not banned it but has issued warnings about DHEA-related compounds.
regulatory_status.UK: Classified as anabolic steroid under Misuse of Drugs Act. Illegal to sell or supply.
regulatory_status.Canada: Not approved as natural health product. Classified as controlled substance.
regulatory_status.Australia: Schedule 4 (prescription only). Banned from importation and OTC sale.
regulatory_status.New Zealand: Classified as prescription medicine. Banned from dietary supplements.
